questions.question: When the United States flag is displayed on a stage, where is it positioned from the perspective of the audience?
options: On the audience's right
On the audience's left
Centered behind the speaker
On either side at the speaker's discretion

questions.question: When the United States flag is displayed with another flag against a wall from crossed staffs, where should the U.S. flag be placed?
options: On the left, with its staff behind the other flag's staff
On the right (the flag's own right), with its staff in front of the other flag's staff
In the center, elevated above the other flag
On either side, as long as it is higher than the other flag

questions.question: When the United States flag is flown with flags radiating from a central point and no foreign flags are displayed, where is the U.S. flag placed?
options: On the far right of the display
On the far left of the display
In the center and at the highest point of the group
At the lowest point to show accessibility

questions.question: What does the term 'half-staff' mean regarding the United States flag?
options: The flag is positioned one-quarter of the distance from the top of the staff
The flag is positioned one-half the distance between the top and bottom of the staff
The flag is folded in half while on the staff
The flag is flown on a staff that is half the normal height

questions.question: When the United States flag is flown at half-staff, what must happen before the flag is lowered to half-staff?
options: A moment of silence must be observed
The national anthem must be played
The flag must first be hoisted to the peak for an instant
A salute must be rendered by all present

questions.question: The United States flag should never be displayed with the union down, except as a signal of what?
options: A call for reinforcements
Mourning for a fallen service member
Dire distress in instances of extreme danger to life or property
Surrender during wartime

questions.question: When the United States flag is no longer a fitting emblem for display, how should it be destroyed?
options: By shredding and recycling
In a dignified way, preferably by burning
By burying it with full military honors
By returning it to a government facility for disposal

questions.question: According to the chapter, a folded flag is considered what, and therefore does not require a salute?
options: Retired
Secured
Cased
Stowed

questions.question: In the order of precedence for service flags, which service has the earliest date and therefore receives the position of honor?
options: U.S. Army (14 June 1775)
National Guard (13 December 1635)
U.S. Navy (13 October 1775)
U.S. Marine Corps (10 November 1775)

questions.question: A distinguished visitor (DV) may be defined as any of the following EXCEPT:
options: A General or Flag Officer
A government official with rank equivalent to a Brigadier General or higher
Any field grade officer visiting an installation
A visitor or group designated by the commander

questions.question: When walking, riding, or sitting with a senior person, where should the junior person be positioned?
options: To the senior's right
To the senior's left
Directly behind the senior
Directly in front of the senior

questions.question: When entering an automobile, military personnel enter in what order of rank?
options: Seniors enter first and juniors enter last
All enter simultaneously regardless of rank
Juniors enter first, seniors enter last
The driver determines the order of entry

questions.question: When reporting to an officer indoors, at approximately how many paces from the officer or desk should you halt and salute?
options: One pace
Two paces
Three paces
Four paces

questions.question: When in formation, who salutes and acknowledges salutes for those in the formation?
options: Each individual member
The most senior enlisted member
The person in charge of the formation
The first person to notice the approaching officer

questions.question: When wearing physical training gear outdoors, Airmen are required to salute during which events?
options: Any time they encounter a senior officer
Reveille and retreat
All official formations and ceremonies
Saluting is never required in physical training gear

questions.question: What is the one significant difference between a dining-in and a dining-out?
options: A dining-in is informal while a dining-out is formal
A dining-out allows nonmilitary spouses, friends, and civilians to attend, while a dining-in is for military members only
A dining-in involves a seated dinner while a dining-out is a reception format
A dining-out requires mess dress while a dining-in allows service dress

questions.question: The present dining-in format had its beginnings in the Air Corps when which General held his famous 'wingdings'?
options: General Curtis LeMay
General Carl Spaatz
General Henry H. 'Hap' Arnold
General Billy Mitchell

questions.question: Induction into the Order of the Sword is described as the highest honor and tribute that can be bestowed by whom?
options: Any commissioned officer
The Secretary of the Air Force
An enlisted member
The Chief of Staff of the Air Force

questions.question: The retreat ceremony serves a twofold purpose. What are those two purposes?
options: It signals the start of the duty day and honors fallen service members
It signals the end of the official duty day and serves as a ceremony for paying respect to the United States flag
It recognizes unit achievements and transitions the guard
It honors retiring members and closes the installation for the day

questions.question: During a drill command, the command 'Flight, HALT' consists of what two parts?
options: 'Flight' is the command of execution, 'HALT' is the preparatory command
'Flight' is the informational command, 'HALT' is the supplementary command
'Flight' is the preparatory command, 'HALT' is the command of execution
'Flight' is the mass command, 'HALT' is the preparatory command

questions.question: The flight formation is the most practical drill group and is composed of at least two, but no more than how many elements?
options: Three
Four
Five
Six

questions.question: At the position of attention, the feet are turned out equally to form what degree angle?
options: 30-degree angle
45-degree angle
60-degree angle
90-degree angle

questions.question: At the position of parade rest, the heels are how many inches apart as measured from the inside of the heels?
options: 6 inches
10 inches
12 inches
15 inches

questions.question: Which rest position requires members to keep the right foot in place and maintain silence, but allows relaxing in a standing position?
options: Parade rest
At ease
Rest
Fall out

questions.question: The Order of the Sword ceremony was revised, updated, and adopted by the USAF in what year to recognize senior officers (Colonel or above) and civilian equivalents?
